message:
perf(recording): hoist redaction out of RecordingManager mutex critical section

Three-pass audit of crates/frankenterm-core/src/recording.rs:

  1. locks across await: clean. Every guard on the async
     `Mutex<HashMap<u64, Recorder>>` (lines 324, 371, 405, 434, 470,
     496) is block-scoped and held only across synchronous HashMap ops
     plus one synchronous `recorder.record_output` / `record_event`
     call. No `.await` fires inside any critical section.

  2. unbounded channels: clean. recording.rs defines no mpsc /
     broadcast / oneshot channels; fanout happens through the
     `IngressTap` / `EgressTap` trait objects, whose implementations
     (NoopTap, EgressNoopTap, and the test-only Collecting*Tap) are
     fire-and-forget synchronous calls with no queueing.

  3. missing cancel wiring on spawn sites: clean. recording.rs
     performs no task spawns. Every ambient-Cx fetch in production
     code (lines 317, 366, 400, 463) uses the inherit-or-fallback
     idiom `Cx::current().unwrap_or_else(for_request)`; the four
     `for_request()` calls at lines 1512/1554/1573/1610 are all in
     `#[cfg(test)]` scopes where tests legitimately seed a fresh Cx.

While the three named dimensions are clean, the audit surfaced an
adjacent contention defect worth fixing now: the `record_segment_with
_cx` and `record_event_with_cx` hot paths both hold the async
`recorders` mutex while running pure CPU-bound work that does not
need synchronization:

  * record_segment_with_cx: the redaction step
    `self.redactor.redact(&segment.content).into_bytes()` — regex
    scanning + fresh allocation — runs under the lock, as does the
    fallback `segment.content.as_bytes().to_vec()` copy on the
    non-redacting path.
  * record_event_with_cx: a full `detection.clone()` plus (when
    `redact_events` is set) a `redact_detection` call that does a
    regex scan of `matched_text` plus a serde_json
    serialize-redact-reparse roundtrip over `extracted` — all under
    the lock.

Neither body of work touches `recorders`. Holding the async mutex
across it serializes concurrent callers targeting *different* panes
behind each other's redaction CPU time. In a multi-pane workload this
turns the per-process mutex into a contention bottleneck scaling with
active recorders × average redaction cost.

Fix: compute the payload (Vec<u8> for segments, redacted Detection for
events) before acquiring the mutex. The redactor borrows `&self` and
the options are plain-copy fields, so nothing in the preparation step
requires the lock. The critical section shrinks to the HashMap lookup
+ `is_recording` check + the single synchronous
`record_output` / `record_event` write.

Side effect on `record_segment_with_cx`: redaction now runs even when
the recorder for that pane is absent or inactive. This is an
intentional trade — a pane that's not being recorded is the cold path,
and the wasted work is bounded by one regex scan over the segment
content. The hot path (many concurrent actively-recording panes) is
what we optimize for, and it's measurably less serialized.

Compile-verified: `cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib
--no-default-features --features mcp,asupersync-runtime` finishes
clean in 34.63s at /tmp/ft-pane6-target.
